hpo2vec.label: Cushing syndrome due to ectopic ACTH secretion

mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: hypertension, obesity, moon facies, thinning of arms and legs, gynocomastia, hirsutism, acne, easy bruising, purple striae, recurrent infections, osteopenia

renal involvement with proteinuria or glomerulosclerosis, angiokeratomas, cornea verticillata, hearing impairment, cardiac symptoms such as arrhythmias or left ventricular hypertrophy, hypohidrosis, paresthesia, chronic pain, gastrointestinal disturbances including diarrhea, constipation, bloating, and intussusception

progressive neurological deterioration characterized by vertical supranuclear gaze palsy, pyramidal signs, myoclonus, seizures, deafness, cirrhosis, splenomegaly, pulmonary insufficiency, and retinitis pigmentosa
mental retardation, eunuchoid proportions, marfanoid habitus, joint laxity, lens dislocation, tendon contractures, characteristic odor, homocystinuria, increased risk of vascular complications

short stature, coarsening of facial features, skeletal changes, kyphoscoliosis, hernia, respiratory problems, valvular heart defects, sensorineural hearing loss, dental anomalies, delayed sexual maturation

round face, thickened skin, purpuric rashes, lymphadenopathy, hepatosplenomegaly, bowed long bones, vertebral column lesions, uveitis, iridocyclitis, optic nerve damage, neuropsychiatric manifestations

multiple café au lait spots, freckling in axillae and inguinal regions, unilateral or bilateral Lisch nodules, scoliosis, short stature, macrocephaly, learning difficulties, speech disorders, optic glioma, peripheral nervous system tumors

bilaterally involved vestibuloacoustic nerves causing severe sensorineural hearing loss, schwannomas involving cranial nerves other than VIII, meningeal fibromas, cafe-au-lait macules, cutaneous neurolemmas, Langerhans cell histiocytosis
emphysema before age 45 years, bronchiectasis, nasal polyposis, asthma, liver disease, jaundice, elevated serum amylase levels, decreased alpha-1 antitrypsin level in plasma, normal chest radiograph
fever attacks lasting several days accompanied by abdominal pain, arthralgia, erysipelas-like erythema, hemorrhage into organs, amyloidosis, inflammation of eyes, ears, and lungs
